title: The Hidden Cost of AI: Tech Giants Face Rising Emissions
subtitle: As artificial intelligence grows, companies like Google and Amazon struggle to meet their climate goals.

summary.en: Major technology companies are seeing a sharp increase in their carbon emissions due to the rapid growth of artificial intelligence. Google and Amazon have both reported significant rises in energy use as they build more data centers. These facilities are needed to power complex AI systems, which require massive amounts of electricity. Experts warn that this trend makes it harder for these companies to reach their long-term environmental targets. A recent UN report highlights that data centers now consume as much energy as some entire nations.
summary.zh: 由於人工智慧的快速發展，大型科技公司的碳排放量正急劇增加。Google 與亞馬遜在建設更多資料中心以支援複雜的人工智慧系統時，電力消耗量皆大幅上升。專家警告，此趨勢使這些公司更難達成其長期環境目標。聯合國近期的一份報告強調，資料中心的耗電量已相當於某些國家的總用電量。

paragraphs.en: The rapid growth of artificial intelligence (AI) is changing the way we live and work, but it is also creating a significant environmental challenge. As tech companies rush to build the infrastructure needed to support AI, their energy use has increased sharply. Recent reports from Google and Amazon show that their carbon emissions—the gases released into the air that contribute to global warming—have risen significantly over the past year.
paragraphs.zh: 人工智慧的快速發展正在改變我們的生活與工作方式，但也帶來了嚴峻的環境挑戰。隨著科技公司爭相建立支援人工智慧所需的基礎設施，其能源消耗量也急劇增加。根據 Google 與亞馬遜 (Amazon) 最近的報告顯示，過去一年來，這兩家公司的碳排放量（即排放至大氣中、導致全球暖化的氣體）皆顯著上升。

paragraphs.en: According to Deutsche Welle (DW), Google reported a 16% annual jump in its carbon emissions in its 2025 report. Amazon also shared its latest figures, which showed an 18% increase compared to the previous year. When looking back to 2019, the numbers are even higher. Google’s emissions have risen by 82% since that time, while Amazon’s have grown by 58%. To put this in perspective, Amazon’s total emissions are now higher than those of entire countries like Austria or Greece.
paragraphs.zh: 根據德國之聲 (Deutsche Welle) 的報導，Google 在其二零二五年報告中指出，該公司的碳排放量年增率達百分之十六。亞馬遜也公布了最新數據，顯示其排放量較前一年增加了百分之十八。若回溯至二零一九年，數據增幅更為驚人：Google 的排放量自當時起已成長百分之八十二，而亞馬遜則成長了百分之五十八。作為對比，亞馬遜目前的總排放量甚至高於奧地利或希臘等整個國家。

paragraphs.en: These companies are investing heavily in data centers, which are large buildings filled with powerful computers that store information and process AI tasks. These centers require a constant supply of electricity to run and stay cool. Google explained that the resources needed to build and operate these centers, as well as the manufacturing of computer chips and servers, are the main reasons for their growing carbon footprint. Amazon noted that its electricity use rose by 34% in the last year, driven by both the data center boom and the need to power its large delivery network.
paragraphs.zh: 這些公司正投入大量資金建設資料中心，這些大型建築內裝設了強大的電腦，用於儲存資訊並處理人工智慧任務。這些中心需要持續不斷的電力供應以維持運作與冷卻。Google 解釋，建設與營運這些中心所需的資源，以及電腦晶片與伺服器的製造過程，是導致其碳足跡不斷擴大的主因。亞馬遜則指出，受惠於資料中心熱潮以及龐大物流配送網路的用電需求，該公司的電力消耗在去年增加了百分之三十四。

paragraphs.en: This trend has created a difficult situation for the companies' climate goals. Both businesses have promised to reduce their impact on the environment. Amazon has pledged to reach "net-zero" carbon emissions by 2040, meaning they will remove as much carbon from the atmosphere as they produce. Google has a goal to cut its 2019-level emissions in half by 2030. However, leaders at both companies now admit that these targets are becoming much harder to reach.
paragraphs.zh: 這種趨勢使這些公司的氣候目標陷入困境。兩家企業皆曾承諾要降低對環境的影響。亞馬遜承諾在二零四零年達成「淨零」碳排放，意即從大氣中移除的碳量將與其產生的碳量相等。Google 的目標則是到二零三零年時，將排放量減至二零一九年水準的一半。然而，兩家公司的領導階層現在皆坦言，這些目標正變得越來越難以達成。

paragraphs.en: Kate Brandt, Google’s chief sustainability officer, explained that the company’s AI infrastructure is growing faster than the power grid is becoming "decarbonized." Decarbonization refers to the process of switching from fossil fuels like coal and oil to cleaner energy sources like wind or solar power. Similarly, Kara Hurst, Amazon’s chief sustainability officer, acknowledged that there are new hurdles to meeting their environmental benchmarks. She noted that while the company wants to move quickly, the high demand for AI technology might slow down their progress.
paragraphs.zh: Google 永續長凱特·布蘭特 (Kate Brandt) 解釋，該公司的人工智慧基礎設施成長速度，已超越了電力網「去碳化」的進程。去碳化是指從煤炭與石油等化石燃料，轉向風能或太陽能等更潔淨能源的過程。同樣地，亞馬遜永續長卡拉·赫斯特 (Kara Hurst) 也承認，在達成環境基準方面出現了新的障礙。她指出，儘管公司希望快速推進，但人工智慧技術的高需求可能會拖慢他們的進度。

paragraphs.en: Despite these challenges, both companies are trying to show that they are still committed to the environment. Google claims that its efforts to use cleaner energy have been successful, stating that its 2025 emissions would have been five times higher without these initiatives. Amazon also pointed out that its data centers are more energy-efficient than the industry average. However, the scale of the problem remains large. A report published by the United Nations in June 2026 found that if all data centers in the world were a single country, they would rank 11th globally for electricity consumption. This puts them in a position between France and Saudi Arabia.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管面臨這些挑戰，兩家公司仍試圖展現其對環境的承諾。Google 聲稱其使用潔淨能源的努力已見成效，並表示若無這些措施，其二零二五年的排放量將會是現在的五倍。亞馬遜也指出，其資料中心的能源效率優於產業平均水準。然而，問題的規模依然龐大。聯合國於二零二六年六月發布的一份報告發現，若將全球所有資料中心視為一個國家，其電力消耗量將位居全球第十一名，介於法國與沙烏地阿拉伯之間。

paragraphs.en: As AI continues to expand, the tension between technological progress and climate responsibility is likely to grow. The challenge for these tech giants is to find a way to balance the massive energy needs of modern computing with their promises to protect the planet. For now, the rapid rise in emissions serves as a reminder that every new digital tool has a real-world cost in energy and resources. Whether these companies can find a way to grow without harming the environment remains a key question for the future of the technology industry.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著人工智慧持續擴張，科技進步與氣候責任之間的緊張關係可能會加劇。這些科技巨頭面臨的挑戰，在於如何平衡現代運算所需的龐大能源需求，以及他們保護地球的承諾。目前，排放量的快速上升提醒著我們，每一項新的數位工具在現實世界中都伴隨著能源與資源的代價。這些公司能否在不傷害環境的前提下持續成長，仍是未來科技產業的關鍵課題。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To operate or function, especially in reference to machines or systems.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「跑步」，這裡指機器或系統的運作。文中：These centers require a constant supply of electricity to run and stay cool.
常見作「駕駛」，這裡指某事是由特定因素造成的。文中：Amazon noted that its electricity use rose by 34% in the last year, driven by both the data center boom and the need to power its large delivery network.

keyTerms.definitionEn: The size, level, or extent of something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「秤」或「鱗片」，這裡指問題的大小或範圍。文中：However, the scale of the problem remains large.

sources.name: Deutsche Welle (DW) Top
sources.title: Google, Amazon emissions rise sharply, driven by AI boom

quiz.question: According to the article, what is the primary reason for the increase in Google's carbon footprint?

options.A: The company's reliance on coal and oil for its delivery networks.
options.B: The resources required to build and operate data centers and manufacture hardware.
options.C: A failure to implement energy-efficient cooling systems in its global offices.
options.D: The rapid expansion of the company's retail and shipping operations.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，Google 明確指出其碳足跡增加的主要原因是建設與運作數據中心所需的資源，以及電腦晶片與伺服器的製造過程。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章將貨運網路的電力需求歸咎於 Amazon 而非 Google。選項 C 文中未提及辦公室冷卻問題。選項 D 雖然提到 Amazon 有物流需求，但並非 Google 碳排放增加的主因。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the relationship between AI development and the companies' environmental goals?

options.A: AI development has made it easier for companies to reach their net-zero targets.
options.B: Tech companies are planning to stop AI development to prioritize the environment.
options.C: The pace of AI infrastructure growth is currently outpacing the transition to clean energy.
options.D: The companies have successfully decoupled AI growth from their electricity consumption.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第五段，Google 的永續長 Kate Brandt 指出，AI 基礎設施的成長速度快於電網「去碳化」（decarbonized）的速度，這暗示了科技進步的速度超前了環保能源轉型的速度，導致目標難以達成。選項 A 與事實相反；選項 B 文中並未提到停止開發；選項 D 錯誤，因為文中強調電力消耗與 AI 成長是正相關的，並未脫鉤。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the word 'hurdles' mean in the context of the companies' environmental commitments?

options.A: Physical barriers used to protect data centers.
options.B: Technological advancements that speed up progress.
options.C: Difficulties or obstacles that prevent success.
options.D: Financial investments made to offset carbon emissions.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。在第五段中，Kara Hurst 提到「new hurdles to meeting their environmental benchmarks」，上下文談論的是達成環保目標的困難，因此 hurdles 在此處意指「障礙」或「難題」。選項 A 是字面上的誤解（柵欄）；選項 B 與語意相反；選項 D 雖然文中提到投資，但 hurdles 本身指的是阻礙而非投資行為。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article regarding the tech industry and the environment?

options.A: Tech companies are intentionally ignoring environmental damage to maximize profits.
options.B: The rapid rise of AI is creating a significant conflict between technological progress and climate responsibility.
options.C: Data centers are the most environmentally friendly way to store information globally.
options.D: International laws have successfully forced tech companies to cut emissions by half.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章從頭到尾都在探討 AI 發展帶來的能源需求，以及這與科技巨頭環保承諾之間的矛盾，並在結尾處總結這是一個「科技進步與氣候責任」之間的緊張關係。選項 A 過於主觀且文章未指控蓄意破壞；選項 C 與文章提到的高耗電量事實矛盾；選項 D 文中提到目標難以達成，並未說明已透過法律成功減排。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
